seoTitle: How to Create a Footnote in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Insert footnotes in Google Docs with the Insert menu or a keyboard shortcut, then format citation text at the base of the page for academic writing.
intro: Footnotes are essential for academic papers, legal documents, and anything requiring precise source attribution. This guide shows how to place a footnote marker at your cursor, use the keyboard shortcut for speed, and format the citation text that appears automatically at the bottom of the page, all without breaking your document's existing layout.

steps.heading: Position your cursor
steps.body: Click exactly where you want the footnote marker to appear within your paragraph text.

steps.heading: Insert the footnote
steps.body: Go to Insert, then Footnote, to place a sequential superscript number at your cursor's location.

steps.heading: Use the keyboard shortcut
steps.body: Press Ctrl+Alt+F on Windows/ChromeOS or Command+Option+F on macOS to add footnotes faster without navigating the menu each time.

steps.heading: Add your citation text
steps.body: Type your source reference or note into the automatically generated space at the bottom margin of the page.

steps.heading: Format footnote typography
steps.body: Adjust font size and paragraph spacing for your footnote text so it matches your document's citation standards.

steps.heading: Manage or remove footnotes
steps.body: Delete a footnote marker to remove both the number and its corresponding note, and remaining footnotes will renumber automatically.

faq.question: Do footnote numbers update automatically if I add more?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Docs automatically renumbers footnotes sequentially as you add or remove them throughout the document.

faq.question: Can I use footnotes for something other than academic citations?
faq.answer: Yes, footnotes work well for any supplementary note, definition, or reference you want to keep separate from the main text.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a footnote by mistake?
faq.answer: Deleting the superscript marker in the text removes the corresponding footnote entirely, and you'd need to reinsert it if that was accidental.

faq.question: Can I change the footnote font without changing my body text?
faq.answer: Yes, footnote text can be formatted independently, so you can set a smaller size or different font just for citations.
